title: carlina frigida
intro: Carlina frigida, also known as the Arctic daisy or winter alyssum, is a perennial plant species native to the Arctic tundra regions of Europe and Asia, characterized by its small white flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high content of essential oils and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Carlina frigida are believed to provide health benefits such as wound healing, reduced inflammation, and improved respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including colds, coughs, and digestive issues.
